name: Fruit Scones
recipeIngredient: 6 tablespoons Cold butter, cut into small pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix heavier than plain scones. Make them slightly smaller than normal and serve without jam or cream.
2. Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Lightly grease a large baking sheet; set aside.
3. Sift flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt into a large bowl. Stir in sugar. With your fingers, rub in butter pieces until the mixture is crumbly.
4. Mix in raisins. Using a spatula, stir in egg, yogurt, and lemon peel and blend well to make a dough that barely holds together (you may need to press dough together with your hands).
5. Turn out onto a floured surface. Roll out with a floured rolling pin or pat dough with your hands to form a round about 1/2-inch thick.
6. Cut into rounds using a 1 1/2 inch fluted or plain cookie cutter. Place 1 to 1 1/2 inches apart on baking sheet; brush tops lightly with milk.
7. Bake for 10 to 12 minutes or until scones are well risen and golden. Transfer to a wire rack to cool for 5 minutes.
8. Split and serve warm with butter. Makes about 18 scones.
